Original Requirement Text: The application shall communicate with Firefly III via REST API (v1) exclusively through the shared firefly-python-api package, which provides an authenticated session, URL validation, and configuration loading
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall communicate with Firefly III via REST API (v1) exclusively through the shared firefly-python-api package, which provides an authenticated session, URL validation, and configuration loading
Rationale: Meets all review criteria. Interpreted as an intentional architectural constraint; the relative clause is descriptive context, not a second obligation.

Original Requirement Text: The analysis time window shall be configurable (default: 24 months)
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall read the analysis time window from configuration, with a default of 24 months.
Rationale: Obligation placed on the artefact ("the analysis time window") in passive form; reformulated with the application as active subject per ISO 29148 5.2.4. Default value preserved from the original.

Original Requirement Text: The application shall support the frequencies: monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, yearly, irregular
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall classify each recurring payment pattern into exactly one of the frequencies monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, yearly, or irregular.
Rationale: Weak verb "support" replaced with "classify", which matches the intent evident in UC2 step 4. List fully expanded and made exclusive to be verifiable.

Original Requirement Text: The confidence threshold for automatic approval shall be configurable
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall read the confidence threshold for automatic approval from configuration.
Rationale: Obligation placed on the artefact ("the confidence threshold") in passive form; reformulated with the application as active subject. The configuration parameter HIGH_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD in the same document appears to realize this requirement.

Original Requirement Text: The application shall check whether a bill already exists before creating it
Suggested Reformulation: When the application is about to create a bill, the application shall verify whether a matching bill already exists in Firefly III before creating the bill.
Missing Information: Matching criterion for "already exists" (name only, name plus amount, etc.) is undefined
Rationale: Trigger made explicit in P2 form. Pronoun "it" resolved. The duplicate-matching criterion is undefined, so a test case cannot be fully derived; criterion must be defined by the requirement owner.

Original Requirement Text: The amount range (min/max) shall be calculated with a configurable margin
Suggested Reformulation: When the application creates a bill, the application shall compute the bill amount range (minimum and maximum) by applying a configurable margin to the estimated amount.
Rationale: Passive obligation on the artefact ("the amount range"); reformulated with the application as active subject and trigger made explicit. Baseline for the margin (estimated amount) inferred from UC4; requires owner confirmation.

Original Requirement Text: Dry-run mode shall be supported via a flag and shall not write anything to Firefly III
Suggested Reformulation: When the application is started with the dry-run flag, the application shall activate dry-run mode.
Rationale: Original combined two obligations (mode activation and write inhibition); decomposed per R4. Weak verb "supported" and passive artefact subject corrected. Flag name --dry-run per UC5.

Original Requirement Text: Dry-run mode shall be supported via a flag and shall not write anything to Firefly III
Suggested Reformulation: While dry-run mode is active, the application shall not write any data to Firefly III.
Rationale: Second obligation from the decomposition. The negative form is a legitimate scope exclusion (inhibition requirement) and is intentionally kept negative; NEGATIVE-REQUIREMENT not tagged per R12.

Original Requirement Text: Results shall be exportable to CSV or JSON
Suggested Reformulation: Upon user request, the application shall export the analysis results to CSV format or JSON format.
Rationale: Passive obligation on the artefact ("results"); reformulated with the application as active subject. Trigger "upon user request" inferred from UC5; requires owner confirmation.

Original Requirement Text: The application shall log all API calls and their outcomes
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall log all API calls and their outcomes
Rationale: Meets all review criteria. The quantifier "all API calls" makes applicability explicit; "and" joins items of one logging obligation, not two obligations.

Original Requirement Text: Configuration shall be handled via a .env file or environment variables
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall read its configuration from a .env file or from environment variables.
Missing Information: Precedence order when both a .env file and environment variables define the same parameter is undefined
Rationale: Passive artefact subject and weak verb "handled" corrected; the application made the active subject. Precedence between the two sources should be defined.

Original Requirement Text: The application shall support an include list and an exclude list for categories
Suggested Reformulation: When a category include list is configured, the application shall include only transactions whose category matches the include list in the analysis.
Rationale: Weak verb "support" gave no verifiable effect; behavior taken from UC6 alternative flow. Decomposed into include-list and exclude-list obligations per R4.

Original Requirement Text: The application shall support an include list and an exclude list for categories
Suggested Reformulation: When a category exclude list is configured, the application shall exclude transactions whose category matches the exclude list from the analysis.
Rationale: Second obligation from the decomposition, behavior per UC6 alternative flow step 2.

Original Requirement Text: Transactions whose category appears in the include list shall receive a configurable confidence boost
Suggested Reformulation: When a transaction's category appears in the include list, the application shall increase that transaction's confidence score by the configured category confidence boost.
Rationale: Obligation placed on the artefact ("transactions"); reformulated with the application as active subject and the category condition moved into the trigger clause per ISO 29148 5.2.4.

Original Requirement Text: The category name shall be shown in the table view and included in the bill name if unique per payee
Suggested Reformulation: The web UI shall display the category name in the table view.
Rationale: Original combined a display obligation and a naming obligation; decomposed per R4. It is ambiguous whether "if unique per payee" qualifies both clauses or only the bill name; interpreted as qualifying only the bill name, per UC6. Requires owner confirmation.

Original Requirement Text: The category name shall be shown in the table view and included in the bill name if unique per payee
Suggested Reformulation: When the category is unique per payee, the application shall include the category name in the bill name.
Rationale: Second obligation from the decomposition. Condition "unique per payee" retained as trigger; its exact meaning (exactly one category among the payee's transactions) should be confirmed.

Original Requirement Text: The behavior for uncategorized transactions shall be configurable (include/exclude/neutral)
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall process uncategorized transactions according to the configured behavior, where the configured behavior is one of include, exclude, or neutral.
Missing Information: Definition of the "neutral" behavior is not given
Rationale: Passive obligation on the artefact ("the behavior"); reformulated with the application as active subject. "neutral" is undefined in the specification and must be defined to be verifiable.

Original Requirement Text: The application shall expose a web UI via a built-in HTTP server
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall expose a web UI via a built-in HTTP server
Rationale: Meets all review criteria. Interpreted as an intentional architectural constraint on delivery mechanism.

Original Requirement Text: The web UI shall fetch existing categories from the Firefly III API and present them as multiselect lists
Suggested Reformulation: When the web UI page is loaded, the web UI shall fetch the existing categories from the Firefly III API and display them as multiselect lists.
Rationale: Trigger absent in the original; "on page load" taken from UC6 primary flow step 1. Fetch-and-display treated as one obligation chain, not decomposed. Subsystem "web UI" is an acceptable subject per R11.

Original Requirement Text: Analysis results shall be shown in a sortable table with inline editing of amount, frequency, and start date
Suggested Reformulation: When an analysis completes, the web UI shall display the analysis results in a sortable table.
Rationale: Original combined display and editing obligations; decomposed per R4. Passive artefact subject corrected to the web UI. Trigger inferred from UC3; requires owner confirmation.

Original Requirement Text: Analysis results shall be shown in a sortable table with inline editing of amount, frequency, and start date
Suggested Reformulation: The web UI shall accept inline edits of amount, frequency, and start date for each analysis result row.
Rationale: Second obligation from the decomposition; editing capability per UC3 step 4 made an explicit obligation of the web UI.

Original Requirement Text: The web UI shall show the outcome of bill creation (created / already exists / error) without a page reload
Suggested Reformulation: When bill creation completes, the web UI shall display the outcome per bill (created, already exists, or error) without reloading the page.
Rationale: Trigger only implicit ("the outcome of bill creation"); made explicit in P2 form per UC3 step 6. Enumerated outcomes fully expanded.

Original Requirement Text: Dry-run mode shall be activatable via a toggle in the web UI
Suggested Reformulation: When the user enables the dry-run toggle in the web UI, the web UI shall activate dry-run mode.
Rationale: Passive capability form ("shall be activatable") with the mode as subject; reformulated with the web UI as active subject and the user action as trigger per ISO 29148 5.2.4.

Original Requirement Text: Export of analysis results (CSV/JSON) shall be triggerable via a button in the web UI
Suggested Reformulation: When the user clicks the export button in the web UI, the application shall export the analysis results to CSV format or JSON format.
Rationale: Passive capability form with the export action as subject; reformulated with the application as active subject and the user action as trigger.

Original Requirement Text: The application shall cache categories, bills, transactions, and payees as JSON files on disk
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall cache categories, bills, transactions, and payees as JSON files on disk
Rationale: Meets all review criteria. "and" joins the items of one caching obligation, not separate obligations; not decomposed per R4.

Original Requirement Text: TTL shall be separately configurable per data set
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall read a separate cache TTL from configuration for each cached data set (categories, bills, transactions, payees).
Rationale: Passive obligation on the artefact ("TTL"); reformulated with the application as active subject. "per data set" made explicit by expanding the data sets from FR-21 and the UC7 table.

Original Requirement Text: The bills cache shall be invalidated immediately when a new bill is created via UC4
Suggested Reformulation: When the application creates a new bill, the application shall invalidate the bills cache within [VALUE TBD] ms.
Rationale: Passive artefact subject corrected. "immediately" is not measurable; a latency bound is required, or the requirement owner may confirm that synchronous invalidation within the creation operation is intended. Cross-reference to UC4 replaced with the concrete event.

Original Requirement Text: The web UI shall expose a "Clear cache" button that removes all cached data
Suggested Reformulation: The web UI shall display a "Clear cache" button.
Rationale: Original combined a UI element obligation and a behavioral obligation in a relative clause; decomposed per R4.

Original Requirement Text: The web UI shall expose a "Clear cache" button that removes all cached data
Suggested Reformulation: When the user clicks the "Clear cache" button, the application shall delete all cached data.
Rationale: Second obligation from the decomposition; behavior per UC7 manual-clear flow, with the application as acting subject and the user click as trigger.

Original Requirement Text: The firefly-python-api package shall read FIREFLY_URL and FIREFLY_TOKEN from environment variables or a .env file and expose a FireflyClient class with a configured requests.Session
Suggested Reformulation: The firefly-python-api package shall read FIREFLY_URL and FIREFLY_TOKEN from environment variables or from a .env file.
Missing Information: Precedence order between environment variables and the .env file is undefined
Rationale: Original combined configuration loading and interface exposure; decomposed per R4. Precedence between sources should be defined (same gap as FR-10).

Original Requirement Text: The firefly-python-api package shall read FIREFLY_URL and FIREFLY_TOKEN from environment variables or a .env file and expose a FireflyClient class with a configured requests.Session
Suggested Reformulation: The firefly-python-api package shall expose a FireflyClient class that provides a configured requests.Session.
Rationale: Second obligation from the decomposition. Naming a concrete implementation type (requests.Session) is a design decision inside a requirement (R7); acceptable only if this document is intentionally also the interface specification for the package. Requires owner confirmation.

Original Requirement Text: The confidence score shall be computed from occurrence count (weight 0.4), interval regularity (weight 0.4), and amount stability (weight 0.2), with an optional category boost; the result shall be clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
Suggested Reformulation: When the application classifies recurring payment patterns, the application shall compute the confidence score as 0.4 times the occurrence score plus 0.4 times the regularity score plus 0.2 times the amount score plus the category boost, and shall clamp the result to the range [0.0, 1.0].
Rationale: Passive obligation on the artefact ("the confidence score"); reformulated with the application as active subject. Computation and clamping treated as one obligation (clamping is part of producing the score). Formula details align with UC2 step 5.

Original Requirement Text: The application shall be written in Python 3.10+
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall be written in Python 3.10+
Rationale: Meets all review criteria. Development constraint in conventional passive constraint form; imposes a verifiable obligation and is reviewed as a normal constraint requirement.

Original Requirement Text: External dependencies shall be kept minimal; requests, python-dotenv, and Flask or FastAPI are sufficient
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall use no external runtime dependencies other than requests, python-dotenv, and Flask or FastAPI.
Missing Information: Final choice between Flask and FastAPI is open
Rationale: "minimal" is subjective; the second clause was interpreted as defining the allowed dependency set and folded into a verifiable closed list. Obligation moved to the application in active form. The Flask/FastAPI alternative leaves the dependency set undetermined.

Original Requirement Text: The application shall handle Firefly III API pagination transparently
Suggested Reformulation: When a Firefly III API response is paginated, the application shall fetch all pages and aggregate the results before returning them to the caller.
Rationale: Weak verb "handle" and subjective adverb "transparently" replaced with concrete, verifiable behavior. Interpretation (fetch all pages, aggregate) requires owner confirmation.

Original Requirement Text: Error handling shall produce clear error messages without stack traces for common errors
Suggested Reformulation: If a common error occurs, then the application shall display an error message that states the cause of the error and shall not include a stack trace in the message.
Missing Information: Definition or list of "common errors"; criteria for what constitutes a "clear" message
Rationale: Obligation placed on "error handling" instead of the application; corrected. "clear" is subjective; approximated with "states the cause", requires owner confirmation. "common errors" must be enumerated to be verifiable. The negative clause is a legitimate exclusion; not tagged NEGATIVE-REQUIREMENT.

Original Requirement Text: Analysis of 24 months of data shall complete in under 60 seconds
Suggested Reformulation: When the user starts an analysis of 24 months of transaction data, the application shall complete the analysis within 60 seconds.
Missing Information: Assumed transaction volume and execution environment for the 60-second bound
Rationale: Obligation placed on "analysis" instead of the application; corrected to active form with explicit trigger. Time bound is measurable but the reference data volume and hardware are unstated, which weakens verifiability.

Original Requirement Text: The web UI shall work without external CDN dependencies (all assets served locally)
Suggested Reformulation: The web UI shall load all of its assets from the local web server and shall not fetch any asset from an external CDN.
Rationale: Weak verb "work" replaced with the concrete obligation from the parenthesis. The negative clause is a legitimate scope exclusion; not tagged NEGATIVE-REQUIREMENT.

Original Requirement Text: The web server shall listen on a configurable port (default: 5000) and configurable bind address (default: 127.0.0.1); to allow access from other machines the bind address shall be settable to 0.0.0.0 via WEB_HOST
Suggested Reformulation: The web server shall listen on a configurable port, with a default port of 5000.
Rationale: Original combined port configuration, bind-address configuration, and a specific bind-address capability; decomposed per R4.

Original Requirement Text: The web server shall listen on a configurable port (default: 5000) and configurable bind address (default: 127.0.0.1); to allow access from other machines the bind address shall be settable to 0.0.0.0 via WEB_HOST
Suggested Reformulation: The web server shall bind to the address configured via WEB_HOST, with a default bind address of 127.0.0.1, including support for the value 0.0.0.0.
Rationale: Second obligation from the decomposition. The third clause of the original (settable to 0.0.0.0) is a special case of configurable bind address and was merged here; it added no independent obligation.

Original Requirement Text: The cache directory shall persist across application restarts
Suggested Reformulation: The application shall retain the cache directory and its contents across application restarts.
Rationale: Obligation placed on the artefact ("the cache directory"); reformulated with the application as active subject per ISO 29148 5.2.4. Note: the ID sequence jumps from NFR-07 to NFR-09; NFR-08 is missing from the specification.

Original Requirement Text: firefly-python-api shall be a standalone, pip-installable package shared with firefly-bank-importer
Suggested Reformulation: firefly-python-api shall be a standalone, pip-installable package shared with firefly-bank-importer
Rationale: Meets all review criteria. Packaging constraint with the package itself as subject; "pip-installable" and "shared with firefly-bank-importer" are verifiable properties.

Original Requirement Text: The shared package shall have no opinionated dependencies beyond requests and python-dotenv
Suggested Reformulation: The firefly-python-api package shall declare no runtime dependencies other than requests and python-dotenv.
Rationale: "opinionated" is subjective and unverifiable; reformulated as a closed dependency list, requires owner confirmation. "The shared package" used as a synonym for firefly-python-api used elsewhere; canonical name applied.

Original Requirement Text: The application does not create categories or budgets in Firefly III
Rationale: Standalone scope constraint stated as fact in the Constraints section, not attached to a specific requirement. Legitimate negative scope boundary per R12; if it shall be binding and verifiable, it should be promoted to a shall-not requirement by the owner.

Original Requirement Text: Transactions with an irregular pattern (confidence below the minimum threshold) are reported but do not result in bill creation without explicit approval
Rationale: Constraint qualifying the behavior of FR-04/FR-05 and UC3 approval flow rather than a standalone obligation. Note a latent terminology issue: it equates "irregular pattern" with "confidence below threshold", while FR-03 and UC2 define irregular by interval, not confidence; owner should reconcile.

Original Requirement Text: The application does not handle deletion or updating of existing bills
Rationale: Standalone scope constraint stated as fact. Legitimate negative scope boundary; if binding, promote to a shall-not requirement or an explicit out-of-scope statement confirmed by the owner.

Missing Information: Definitions of "neutral" behavior, "common errors", "opinionated" dependency, duplicate-matching criterion
Rationale: Dominant binding modal is "shall", used consistently; no modal drift detected. Terminology is largely consistent (application, web UI, Firefly III), but "the shared package" alternates with "firefly-python-api" (NFR-11), and "irregular" is defined by interval in FR-03/UC2 but by confidence in the Constraints section. No term-definition section per ISO 29148 5.2.4; the Actors table partially compensates. NFR numbering gap: NFR-08 is missing.

The input carries no traceability apparatus (no parent/derived-from fields, no RTM references, no origin annotations); traceability was out of scope for this review and no per-row TRACE-MISSING tags were applied. Requirements do informally reference use cases (e.g. FR-23 references UC4), which could be formalized into trace links.
